title: Chapter 26
text: Chapter Twenty- Three T hey walked for hours in the dark, each step harder than the last for Lara. The coat helped with the biting wind, but the temperature dropped further as the night went on, cold seeping through her layers of clothing to chill her skin. She kept her head down to shield her stinging eyes, watching the ground — every inch of it the same dirt, the same grass — slowly creep by . Lara had dealt with cold and wind all her life, but the structures around Cheyenne blocked the worst of it, and even four rickety walls were better than this wide-open nothingness . She pulled up the collar of the coat, clutching it around her neck to seal in some heat. Her fingers were going numb, and her cheeks and nose stung beneath her scarf, which she’d wrapped around her lower face. Her pack sagged; she hopped to kick it up, grasping a strap in one hand to keep it in place . Ronin’s tracks continued through the dirt. She followed them, comforted that the crunching of his boots was only a few feet ahead; lifting her head to check was too much effort, and would only expose her to blasts of stinging dust . “R-R-Ronin!” she called, chin quivering . His steady footfalls stopped, and she risked a glance up to see him turn toward her. His mouth and nose were hidden behind his mask . “What’s wrong ?” “F- Freezing .” “We have to keep moving, Lara. You’re only going to get colder if you stop .” She moved to his side, body shaking. “How m-much farther ?” “A long way.” She heard his frown in his tone. Squinting against the wind, she watched him adjust his packs to shrug out of one sleeve on his coat. He drew Lara into it, wrapping an arm around her shoulders, and his warmth suffused her. She pressed her face to his side and embraced him . Lara’s eyes drifted shut, and she leaned against him. Her legs were stiff and weak after miles of trekking through the Dust. If only she could sleep, just a little … Ronin squeezed her arm. “Come on.” He walked forward, and she forced her legs to move along with him to keep close to his warmth. “This’ll be the hardest night, if we’re lucky .” She swallowed her guilt — he could go all night and through the next day without missing a step, and she was just slowing him down — and focused on walking, on his warmth, on the rustle of fabric as he moved. Anything but how cold and exhausted she was . “Distract me, Ronin .” “What kind of places would you like to see ?” “The ocean,” she replied without hesitation. “I saw a picture in one of the books in the attic.” It had been such a beautiful, vibrant blue . “Pictures don’t do it justice.” He extended his free hand, sweeping it across the entire horizon. “It’s so vast that you’d have to journey for weeks on its waters to reach the other side. Everything is tiny compared to the ocean. Us, all of our worries, needs, problems …” “ Go on .” “All of it is insignificant in the face of something so massive .” “Did you see it? The other side ?” “If I have, I don’t remember. But I know it’s there .” “Is the water really that blue ?” “Sometimes. With shades of green or gray. I think, in some places, it has no color at all. It’s so clear you can see to the bottom .” “I’d like to see them all .” He rubbed his hand up and down her shoulder, generating additional warmth. “That’s no small desire, Lara Brooks .” “We have the world ahead of us, don’t we?” She tilted her head back and smirked up at him, though he couldn’t see it through her scarf . “All of it,” he agreed, “but I reserve the right not to take you to certain parts .” “What parts ?” “The Dust is only one part of this world. A big one, to be certain, and there’s not much worse…but there’s not very much better, either. There are places that would kill you if you went too close .” Lara nuzzled her cheek against him, closing her eyes and calling up the pictures of the ocean. “As long as I’m with you, I don’t care which parts we skip .” Her words drifted away on the wind, and they walked in comfortable silence for a long while. Lara’s eyelids were heavy, and her legs grew more so with each step. His hold on her tightened as she gradually sagged against him . “How did you learn to dance the way you do ?” His question jarred her out of near-sleep, and her tired mind struggled to understand what he was asking . “When I was little, I saw a woman dancing. I spied on her…like you did to me. I didn’t know what to make of it, only that it looked beautiful. So, I started doing it, too. And I felt so…free. I loved it .” “I’ve never seen anything like it. Women dance at places like Kitty’s, but that doesn’t compare to what you do .” “Places like Kitty’s, huh?” A sudden weight formed in her gut, and a fire sparked in her chest. How many women had he watched ? How many of them had he fucked ? “Most settlements have similar establishments. Never held much interest for me. They usually look so…lifeless on stage .” “Probably not so lifeless in bed,” she muttered. She didn’t like this feeling, this jealousy , but it wouldn’t go away. Clenching her teeth, she pulled away. Lara knew she had no right to be upset about his past, but she couldn’t stand the thought of him making love to another person — or bot — as he did with her . Ronin didn’t let her get far before he drew her back against his side. It was difficult to protest, despite her fiery emotions, given the heat he put out . “The last female I coupled with, before you, was another synth. That was four thousand, one hundred and twelve days before I met you. When we finished, we each went our way without a backward glance .” Lara wasn’t surprised that he knew it down to the day, but had it really been that long ? “What about human women?” She didn’t want to know, not really, but the question came out anyway . “There’ve been two, over the years. Both the same situation as with the synth. There was never a connection with any of them .” As easy as it would be to get angry at him, to accuse him of lying about the nature of his past couplings , she couldn’t. He was being honest, and how could she fault him for things that probably happened before she was even born? His mind captured everything , every minute detail of every experience . And, despite the women he had sex with in the past, it was Lara who he’d chosen to be with. He cared for her, and she loved him . “I’m sure I was active before the Black Out, too, though I do not know for how long or what purpose I might have served then . “ “It doesn’t matter, right? The past is in the past.” Saying it out loud helped her remaining tension ease. If she stopped to think about it really hard, she was sure she’d still be jealous of those other women, but that couldn’t be helped. Ronin was hers . “Was the woman you watched different, too?” he asked . “ Different how ?” “You move with emotion. Lose yourself in it. Was she the same ?” “Yeah.” The memories were fuzzy, like they were from a different life. “At Kitty’s, we danced because we had to. That was the only way we could earn credits to buy food. Nobody was there to watch us express our feelings. They just wanted us to shake our tits around .” “I do enjoy your breasts,” Ronin said thoughtfully. Lara smacked his chest. “But it wasn’t your body that first caught my attention. It was the way you moved it. That dance spoke everything in your heart before you said your first fuck off to me .” She laughed, shaking her head. “That’s not the first thing I said to you .” “No. First, you asked who I was. Then it was get out , followed by I know what you are, you’re a — ” “Okay, okay, I get it. I don’t know if that memory of yours is a good thing or not.” She peeked up at him briefly before burying her face in his warmth . “I remember everything that’s happened since I woke. All the pain, the struggles, the terrible scenes. But all the good, too. There’s not nearly as much of that…but it seems to go a lot further .” “Will you remember me, when I’m gone ?” His stride faltered, body going rigid before he stopped. She looked up again to find his eyes on her . “Even through another Black Out, I could never forget you, Lara Brooks .” Her eyes stung, and she couldn’t blame it on the wind this time. She hid her face again, refusing to let him see her cry. Did he love her ? They resumed their walk. He’d successfully distracted her; her mind was awake and racing, examining things he’d said to her, things he’d done for her … Hours more passed, but it wasn’t until the first gray light of dawn touched the sky that Lara faltered, legs giving out beneath her . Ronin held onto her, dipping down to scoop her into his arms without missing a step. “A little farther. There’s a place ahead where we can shelter for a time .” Her lead lolled against him, and she turned into his warmth, throwing an arm over his shoulder. The only response she could manage was a weak nod . She listened to the water sloshing in his bag, let herself be lulled by the steady rhythm of his strides. He was moving faster now that he was carrying her, but his pace didn’t jostle her. It was comforting, focusing only on his heat, on the solidness of his body; her own aches, pains, and weariness were forgotten for a while . * * * A loud pop startled Lara awake. Her eyes went wide, the flickering flames before her blurry until she blinked away her grogginess. She lay on the ground, her head propped on something firm and warm. Brow furrowed, she turned her head to look up at Ronin. Her head was resting on his thigh. He’d removed her scarf, but his coat was over her like a blanket . “You’re safe,” he said, settling a hand on her shoulder . “Water,” she croaked . Ronin twisted the cap off her canteen and held it to her. She cupped the bottom, tilting it back as she lifted her head and drank deeply. The water was heavenly to her parched throat. When she’d had her fill, she pushed it away and dropped her head back into his lap . “ Thanks .” “You should have been drinking more often while we walked .” “Didn’t want to slow you down any more than I already did.” She met his gaze. “Sorry I fell asleep .” “You’ll slow me down a lot more if you get dehydrated, Lara.” He pressed his palm to her cheek. “My goal is to keep you safe, not walk you to death .” She placed her hand over his and smiled. “Could’ve fooled me .” “Some of this will get easier. Your body will grow accustomed to the exertion .” “And how do you know that? I can tell you right now, just laying here, I feel like I fell off a fifty- foot wall .” “Humans adapt. Your bodies change in response to the way you use them .” “Hmm.” Lara closed her eyes. The heat of the fire was amazing on her skin; her last waking memory was of biting cold. “Where are we ?” “One of the few intact buildings out here. Used to house machinery of some sort, I think, but it’s been picked clean over the years. It cuts the wind, at least .” Opening her eyes, Lara examined the place. The ceiling and walls were constructed of metal sheets, parts of it rusted. Wind whistled through the holes eaten in the metal walls, and rays of sunlight streamed through holes in the roof, which meant the sun was overhead. Already midday ? “How long are we staying ?” “Not much longer. You slept for four hours and thirty-seven minutes. We need to get moving soon .” “I was afraid you’d say that.” Four hours wasn’t nearly enough. Dropping her hands, she pushed herself up. Every muscle screamed. Even after days spent scavenging or dancing, she’d never been so exhausted . “Once we reach another settlement, you’ll have time to recover .” “Will it have a bed, like your house?” She could hope, right? She’d spent all her life sleeping on the ground. Less than two weeks in Ronin’s house had spoiled her . “You should eat before we go .” Lara sighed, reaching for her bag. “On it.” She opened it up, removed a strip of dried meat, and took a bite. “Do you think they’ll follow us?” she asked around the mouthful . “It wouldn’t make sense. I broke a rule, and he said to get rid of you. That’s what I’m doing .” Lara frowned. “ What rule ?” “Humans are not to be kept by bots unless he approves of it .” She swallowed her food, and her stomach clenched around it. “That’s…that’s what happened to Tabitha, isn’t it ?” Ronin shifted his gaze to the fire and was silent for several long moments. “ Think so .” “Her bot didn’t get rid of her when he was told. So, Warlord did .” “Or the bot wasn’t deemed valuable enough to the community to earn a warning .” “But you were .” “I brought in materials that Cheyenne requires to remain prosperous. That means something to him .” Lara ran her hand over her hair, sweeping back the strands that had come out of her braid. “He’ll be angry .” “It’ll spare him the frustration of having to deal with me.” He smirked. “He didn’t care for my disregard for the rules, or my disrespectfulness .” Ronin was probably right, but she couldn’t shake the dread that had planted itself in her belly. “Why can’t bots keep humans ?” “Because Warlord hates humans .” “Yeah, I know. Even before you told me about the journal. I wish you would’ve done that sooner, by the way .” “What good would it have done , Lara ?” None. Only more fear could have come from knowing. How could she have stood at the window and looked out over that park without thinking of people dying there, if he’d told her? She’d been afraid enough of gearheads discovering her without knowing they’d killed the people who used to live in those houses. She shifted her gaze to the dwindling fire and chewed her food . “Why does he hate us so much?” she asked, after a while . “I don’t know, Lara.” Ronin shook his head, pressing his lips together. “I don’t know. Intense emotions are new to me. Haven’t hated anyone yet .” “It’s an ugly feeling.” Though her appetite had fled, she took another bite. She needed her strength for this journey. “He allows everyone only a handful of crops, and forces all those people to share one water pump. Couples aren’t allowed more than one child . “Sometimes I wonder why he didn’t just kill us all…but he enjoys it, doesn’t he? The torture. Our terror .” “I don’t know if he enjoys it. He’s…nearly blank. Doesn’t show much .” “But he has to feel something . Why else would he do all of it ?” “Maybe it’s because he feels nothing, and he knows it .” “He feels,” she insisted, recalling his disgust after he raped her. “You do. Why couldn’t he ?” “He’s behind us, now.” Ronin slid beside her and put his arm around her shoulders, drawing her against him. “Whatever he’s done, we must leave it in the past and look to our future .” Lara nodded and leaned her head on his shoulder. What about Gary and Kate, with their second child on the way? They couldn’t just leave it in the past. There was nothing Lara could do for them, nothing she could do for anyone back there. All she could do was look ahead, to the life she’d have with Ronin. To her own happiness . She kissed him. “Thank you for giving me a future to look forward to .” “It’s nothing you haven’t already given me, Lara Brooks .” After eating some more meat and draining the canteen, she forced herself to her feet. Her body threatened to collapse at the exertion, but she didn’t give in. She couldn’t. They had to keep moving. She refilled the canteen from one of the large containers and hooked it on her belt, shrugging out of the heavy coat . Ronin gathered their belongings as Lara wrapped her scarf around her head. Together, they went out into the sweltering heat. Sweat rolled over her skin, trickling between her breasts and down her back. How could anyone deal with such wild variations in temperature every day, without consistent shelter ? As they walked, they passed the twisted, skeletal remains of trees. After having seen the greenery in the Bot District, she could almost imagine what this area might have looked like, the beauty this land must once have possessed . The rugged terrain soon put an end to such musings. The ground dipped and rose with increasing severity, and large, scattered rocks grew more frequent. Several times, her foot caught on the uneven ground, causing her to stumble. She stopped occasionally to catch her breath and take a drink, often at Ronin’s insistence. It took all her focus to keep her feet moving; she had no brainpower left to track her thirst or breathing, on top of that . As they progressed, the dirt gave way to sparse patches of green grass. It was strange to see outside of Cheyenne. Part of her didn’t believe it could exist anywhere else . She stopped at the crest of a tall hill, eyes wide. A stream wound through the small valley below, and along its banks grew clusters of lush grass, trees, and bushes . “It used to look like this all over didn’t it?” she asked, tugging the scarf away from her mouth . “I think it did, in a lot of places .” “Wish I could’ve seen it. Is the rest of the world anything like this spot ?” “There’s a lot more vegetation to the north,” he replied, “but the Dust goes on for a long, long way. Near a thousand miles across in any direction .” A thousand miles? Why did she think she could do this? She was ready to fall over, dead, after a single night . He watched her, as though puzzling out her thoughts. “We’re in the northwestern corner of it now. We’ve a long way to walk, still, but not nearly a thousand miles .” “I can’t walk night and day, Ronin .” “You don’t have to .” “Then we need to get on some kind of schedule for rest. I won’t be able to keep doing this. As well as I’ve been eating, I’m still not prepared for traveling like this .” “Glad I didn’t take you on scrap runs ?” “No. We could’ve taken our time on those. No reason to rush. But this…we’re running from something, even if we think it’s already long behind us .” “Best to treat it that way, for now. Knowing what we left behind …” “I just know I can’t keep up this pace, Ronin. We have to either rest at night and walk during the day, or the other way around. You can’t expect me to do both .” “I don’t expect you to, Lara. You’re worn out, and I’ve taken you away from the only place you’ve ever known. There is uncertainty both before us and behind us. I’m not going to walk you to death when the only reason we left is because I care for you .” She turned away, eyes watering. It wasn’t I love you , but it was pretty damned close . Ronin was in front of her a second later, lifting a hand to gently wipe the tears from her cheeks . “Got sand in my eyes,” she rasped . “Of course you did,” he replied, smiling. “I don’t truly understand what you are experiencing. I can’t. But I see, and hear, and smell, and touch, and I know how the things you feel affect you, even if I don’t fully comprehend what those things are .” Lara offered him a return smile before she pulled her scarf back up. “We’d better get going. Lots of ground to cover, still .” He nodded, surveying the land. “We’ll start veering north, away from the main road. It’ll be rough going, but it’s safer .” Barely holding back a groan, she followed him into the valley . “At least we’ll be going downhill for the next minute or so,” he offered . “Oh? I hadn’t noticed. Are you going to tell me next that we have a big hill to climb ?” Ronin lifted his chin. “ We do .” “I’d kick you, if it would hurt you more than me .” “We’ve already established that I have feelings. Those can be hurt , too .” “But my foot would be hurting more. Besides, you know I’d kiss and make up later .” “Wouldn’t it be more enjoyable for both of us to skip the kick and move directly to the kiss , then ?” “Take my frustrations out on you with sex?” She grinned, the thought sending a rush of excitement through her despite everything. “It might work .” Ronin stopped and turned to face her. His eyes roamed over her; he was considering it ! “You’ve got more walking to do, before you earn that privilege,” he said, displaying a grin of his own . “Oh, really? Well, then, I think I might be too worn out for it by then, anyway.” She walked past him . “I thought you already are, with the way you’ve been talking .” “ Smart ass .” “My ass is, at best, of average intelligence .” Lara shook her head and smirked. His footsteps crunched behind her as he caught up . Soon, she lost count of the hills. The irregular cycle of climbing and descending — short of breath and muscles aflame going up, limbs heavy and rubbery going down — took a new toll on her. She pushed on nonetheless, driven only by pride and stubbornness, but she couldn’t deny her relief and gratitude when he finally took her in his arms and carried her . Once again, his pace increased . Large rock formations came into view as afternoon faded into evening. Lara stared at them in fascination as they finally drew near; the stones were multi-layered and strangely smooth. Her focus was so intent that she jumped at the first boom of thunder . Ronin halted, twisting to look back . “Oh, shit.” Lara’s eyes widened . The sky, normally blanketed with a persistent, yellowish haze, was black with storm clouds in the east. Everything between the clouds and the ground was obscured by dust. It stretched as far as she could see from north to south; a massive, moving wall of dirt . Ronin spun, head turning as though searching, but Lara couldn’t take her eyes off the dust storm. Though it was distant, she knew it was coming toward them. And it was moving fast . Back in Cheyenne, she’d weathered many such storms. People hunkered down in their shacks, tied down doors and shutters, and hoped everything would hold together. If you were well enough to repair your home the next day, it was a victory . She’d never been caught by one in the open . “Hold on , Lara .” She clutched Ronin as he ran .
